sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check if the shape moved this time.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
        print(f"Yellow shape: center=({cr:.0f}, {cc:.0f}), pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Yellow shape: center=(8, 46), pixels=16
Yellow shape: center=(25, 46), pixels=14

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The shape still hasn't moved! Row 6 is also not a movement direction. So none of rows 4-6 are UP.
This means either:
1. The game is broken after game over and I need to reset it
2. The movement mechanics work differently than I thought
Let me try to reset the game by clicking the blue circle with no bars set. Or maybe I need to click one of the small panels at the bottom.
Actually, let me think about this differently. Maybe the issue is that after game over, the game state is corrupted. Let me try clicking the blue circle with NO bars set to see if that resets things.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Reset all bars
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 48, 'col': 39},  # Reset Row 6, col 1
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 48, 'col': 44},  # Reset Row 6, col 2
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 39},  # Reset Row 2, col 1
])
# Click blue circle with no bars set
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 46}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
